cot_prefix: Okay, let's look through the text to find out what we can about the mother. The story starts by mentioning that the mother wanted a flower garden but was too busy raising her five kids: Matthew, Marvin, the twins Alisa and Alan, and Helen. This tells me she has a big family and was a busy mother, which might relate to her being hardworking. It also says that money was tight, and she often told her kids "Do you see a money tree outside? Money doesn't grow on trees, you know." So, she used that proverb to explain that money isn't easy to come by. But later, on Mother's Day, she finds a tree with $100 bills on it. Marvin, one of her sons, put it there as a gift and even helped dig a garden bed. The other kids also contributed to her garden. At the end, she says she's not sure money grows on trees, but she knows love does. So she still doesn't believe money can literally
